evals.prompt: Create a module called `content_indexer` for our Drupal 10 Open Social site. It should have a hook_cron() that finds unindexed content nodes and adds them to a queue for processing. Create a QueueWorker plugin that processes each item by marking it as indexed. The queue worker must have a cron.time setting so items are processed during cron runs. Do NOT process items directly in hook_cron -- use the queue pattern. We want D11 compatibility.
evals.expected_output: A cron+queue module with hook_cron() that adds items to a queue (not processing directly), a QueueWorker plugin with cron.time setting and error handling in processItem(), placed in the correct PSR-4 path
expectations: hook_cron() adds items to queue (calls createItem, does NOT process directly)
QueueWorker plugin has annotation/attribute with cron time setting (cron.time or cron = {"time" = ...})
processItem() handles the actual processing logic
Queue worker has try/catch or error handling in processItem
QueueWorker placed in correct PSR-4 path: src/Plugin/QueueWorker/
The module enables successfully: ddev drush en content_indexer returns exit code 0
E2E: Cron runs without error -- run ddev drush cron and verify exit code 0 (queue worker processes without fatal errors)
